chakobsa_translation: Ruha bela eddem hi uzzul un-raudhiha.
english_dialogue: He shall hold life and death in his hands.
phonetic_transcription: RU-ha be-la ed-DEM hi uz-ZUL un-RAU-dhi-ha.
translation_english: (It is) to-him to-hold life and death in-hands-his.

chakobsa_translation: Ruha leda gefthek sahiimbit qullaha hiyak.
english_dialogue: He shall know your ways as though born to them.
phonetic_transcription: RU-ha le-da GEF-thek sa-HIIM-bit QUL-la-ha hi-yak.
translation_english: (It is) to-him to-know ways-your as-one-who was-born with-them.

english_dialogue: They are not of the desert!

chakobsa_translation: Zeyaashaha qumih. Oma jiladha a-Sich Tabra. Ru vaanar liikasat ziha dimadhagas chos. A-vannat belilii hiyak! Ukairibii unak.
english_dialogue: The night is fading. We must reach Sietch Tabr. The fates of these strangers will be decided there. Until then they have my countenance! My word is on them.
phonetic_transcription: ze-YAA-sha-ha QU-mih. o-ma JI-la-dha a-SICH TAB-ra. ru VAA-nar LIIka-sat zi-ha DI-ma-dha-gas CHOS. a-VAN-nat BE-li-lii hi-yak! u-KAIri-bii u-nak.
translation_english: Is-fading night. (It is) on-us to-reach to-Sietch Tabr. (It is) for fates of-strangers these to-be-decided there. Until-then countenancemy (is) with-them! Promise-my (is) upon-them.

english_dialogue: Tell them to stop hunting my son.

chakobsa_translation: Sah-ifir! Sa-garga! Tasaho! Tasa nichii! E Jamis! Faiza! Hesshyuho! Aga dimash! Uzzulluho!
english_dialogue: By power! By might! Strike him! Strike fast! Hey Jamis! Jump! Make him see! Use your blade! Kill him!
phonetic_transcription: sa-hi-FIR! sa-GAR-ga! TA-sa-ho! TA-sa NI-chii! e JA-mis! FAI-za! HESH-shyu-ho! A-ga DI-mash! uz-ZUL-lu-ho!
translation_english: By-power! By-might! Strike-him! Strike quickly! O Jamis! Jump! Makesee-him! Use blade-your! Kill-him!

english_dialogue: We must get his body's water.
Do you really think he is the One?

phonetic_transcription: OG-go-ri che-SHII, e AK-sha-hii. Coffee good, O friend.

english_dialogue: How can he be the One?
Here. There. Over there. (When pointing to a location.)
She is not fit for the desert!

chakobsa_translation: Ru-che kaah zih? CHIFTHIT! RAABJIL! Sahtha reshme noqadhakaa! ZOTHIB! E Mahdi! Vii she Lisaan al-Gayib?
english_dialogue: What are they doing here? WITCH! SPIES! Leave them to the desert! IMPOSTOR! Mahdi! Are you the Lisan al-Gaib?
phonetic_transcription: ru-CHE KAAH ZIH? CHIF-thit! RAAB-jil! SAH-tha RESH-me NO-qa-dha-kaa!
translation_english: ZO-thib! e MAH-di! VII she li-SAAN al-GA-yib? For-what they here? WITCH! SPIES! Let (the) desert devour-them! IMPOSTOR! O Mahdi! Are you Lisan al-Gaib?

phonetic_transcription: MAR-ra-ka STIL-gar. JI-la. You-have-heard Stilgar. Move.
VIZ-dha-ha CHE JAM-sa? Killed-him who Jamis?

chakobsa_translation: O zihki lefadhas nichaiza hu haraadhim kimme ekkera.
english_dialogue: This has to be done as soon as possible otherwise we lose much of the water.
phonetic_transcription: o ZIH-ki LE-fa-dhas ni-CHAI-za hu ha-RAA-dhim KIM-me EK-ke-ra.
translation_english: (It is) on this to-be-accomplished most-quickly lest we-lose (the) bulk of-the-water.

chakobsa_translation: Bellaha thani Jamas jisne thanath, thedem amhadh, hi suunzaad o qelsshoq akkaarcha ekkera. Rush wa nukah.
english_dialogue: Jamis's body yields thirty-three liters and seven drachms of water. Yours by right.
phonetic_transcription: BEL-la-ha THA-ni JA-mas JIS-ne THA-nath, THE-dem AM-hadh, hi suunZAAD o QELS-SHOQ ak-KAAR-cha EK-ke-ra. RUSH wa NU-kah.
translation_english: Held body of-Jamis half of-body, forty jars, and five-hundred eight drops of-water. For-you by right.

chakobsa_translation: Ekkeri is-Chamas rush. Is-nata ekkerish rum.
english_dialogue: Jamis' water is yours. But your water is ours.
phonetic_transcription: EK-ke-ri is-CHA-mas RUSH. is-NA-ta EK-ke-rish RUM.
translation_english: Water from-Jamis (is) for-you. But your-water (is) for-us.

chakobsa_translation: Unaaha omaiza hajim qumaj. Oma zamashakaa un-yuchkim ozai. Robbuuchkaa is-sih.
english_dialogue: That outsider patrol was too close. We need to engage them further north. Lure them away.
phonetic_transcription: u-NAA-ha o-MAI-za HA-jim QU-maj. O-ma ZA-ma-sha-kaa un-YUCH-kim oZAI. rob-BUUCH-kaa is-SIH.
translation_english: It-was-near too-much patrol outsider. We-must engage-them in-north further. To-lure-them from-here.

chakobsa_translation: Belaalimshii, e Stilgar. Zamha qumju un-yuchkim ozai.
english_dialogue: You have our blessing, Stilgar. Attack the enemy further north.
phonetic_transcription: be-LAA-lim-shii, e STIL-gar. ZAM-ha QUM-ju un-YUCH-kim o-ZAI.
translation_english: We-support-you, O Stilgar. Attack (the) enemy in-north further.

english_dialogue: I need your guidance with the two foreigners.
Give them back to the desert.

chakobsa_translation: E Stilgar, erah chaiya heshkasha shyadhza? Jahhuufahashii minzidhash a-fii.
english_dialogue: Stilgar, how many times have you seen the signs? Your faith is playing tricks on you.
phonetic_transcription: e STIL-gar, E-rah CHAI-ya HESH-ka-sha SHYADH-za? jah-HUU-fa-ha-shii MIN-zi-dhash a-FII.
translation_english: O Stilgar, how-many-times you-have-seen (the) signs? It-istransforming-you your-faith into-moth. (Expression)

chakobsa_translation: So chukkuha Lisaan al-Gayib lek! He, he, he! So a-vadzih!
english_dialogue: The Lisan al-Gaib has not shown his face yet! Ha, ha, ha! Not yet!
phonetic_transcription: SO CHUK-ku-ha li-SAAN al-GA-yib LEK! HE, HE, HE! SO a-vad-ZIH!
translation_english: Not he-has-broken Lisan al-Gaib sand! (Expression) Ha, ha, ha! Not yet!

chakobsa_translation: Vizdhaha Jamsa o zanla vejii. Jaraagaha sa kyurhum. Hajaagaha nichii geftham. Qiraashii dhikadhaho eddemii.
english_dialogue: He killed Jamis in a fair fight. He speaks our language. He will quickly learn our ways. I'm ready to pledge my life for him.
phonetic_transcription: VIZ-dha-ha JAM-sa o ZAN-la ve-JII. ja-RAA-ga-ha sa KYUR-hum. ha-JAAga-ha NI-chii GEF-tham. qi-RAA-shii DHI-ka-dha-ho ED-de-mii.
translation_english: He-killed Jamis in (a) fight fair. He-speaks our language. He-islearning quickly our-ways. I-am-ready to-pledge-him my-life.

chakobsa_translation: Is-nata hu she qoriir minaza oziigi...
english_dialogue: But if you are so blind as to think otherwise...
phonetic_transcription: is-NA-ta hu SHE qo-RIIR MI-na-za o-ZII-gi...
translation_english: But if you are-blind to-think differently...

chakobsa_translation: Shi eddemaadhaha... O erle sahaathahaho reshim obit.
english_dialogue: He may live... For as long as the desert allows.
phonetic_transcription: shi ed-de-MAA-dha-ha... o ER-le sa-HAA-tha-ha-ho RE-shim o-bit.
translation_english: May he-live... For (the) time it-allows-him (the) desert during-it.

chakobsa_translation: Ru resham dimadhag vanraha. Ho cheshii.
english_dialogue: The desert will handle his fate. That's good.
phonetic_transcription: ru RE-sham DI-ma-dhag VAN-ra-ha. HO che-SHII.
translation_english: Will (the) desert decide his-fate. It (is) good.

chakobsa_translation: So vaklaho. Oma hesshyuho vothim. Ho zamit cheshii.
english_dialogue: Don't mock him. He deserves our respect. He's a good fighter.
phonetic_transcription: SO VAK-la-ho. O-ma HESH-shyu-ho VO-thim. ho ZA-mit che-SHII.
translation_english: Do-not mock-him. We-must show-him our-eyes. (Expression) He (is a) fighter good.

english_dialogue: Sayyadina, do you accept her heritage?
phonetic_transcription: E SAY-ya-di-na, VII sa-HAA-lash KAL-ha-ha? O Sayyadina, do you-accept her-heritage?

english_dialogue: Not funny. He's about to lose her.

chakobsa_translation: Hu ho fiidhiir zaiha nah nati naziim so gondho.
english_dialogue: If she's stupid enough to drink that poison that's not our problem.
phonetic_transcription: hu HO fii-DHIIR ZAI-ha NAH NA-ti na-ZIIM SO GON-dho.
translation_english: If she (is) as-stupid to-drink poison that we-carry no guilt.

phonetic_transcription: RUM RU-qa-za HII-li. Let's pray together.

english_dialogue: Here is the Water of Life, which frees the soul.

chakobsa_translation: Hu zaihaash, rush uzzulatha. Hu zaihaash, shi heshyaash.
english_dialogue: If you drink, you shall die. If you drink, you may see.
phonetic_transcription: HU ZAI-haash, rush uz-ZU-la-tha. HU ZAI-haash, shi HESH-yaash.
translation_english: If you-drink, you-will die. If you drink, shi you-see.

english_dialogue: OLD WATERMASTER She did it. She changed the Holy Poison into water!
It is safe now. You can drink it.
phonetic_transcription: Ho le-HII VAD-zih. NI-frish ZAI-ha-ho. It safe now. You-can drink-it.

chakobsa_translation: Hesshyashaho sa vothash birsii! “Ru Kala dhika ekker a-Kwad”! Hi ho vejii! Qithlas-ha!
english_dialogue: You saw it with your OWN EYES! “The Mother shall give the water to the Son”! And she did it! As written!
phonetic_transcription: HESH-shya-sa-ho sa VO-thash bir-SII! “ru KA-la DHI-ka EK-ker aKWAD”! hi HO ve-JII! QITH-las-ha!
translation_english: You-saw-it with your-eyes own! “She-will Mother give water to-Son”! And it (is) true! As-written!

english_dialogue: Blasphemy! The prophecy has been accomplished! He is the One.

chakobsa_translation: O Arraksa gasas isru haiyataha birsiiya!
english_dialogue: Arrakis must be freed by its own people!
phonetic_transcription: o AR-rak-sa GA-sas isru HAI-ya-ta-ha bir-SII-ya!
translation_english: Must Arrakis be-freed by its-people own!

english_dialogue: I'm here to learn your ways.
Kwisatz Haderach, show us the way!
JESSICA (CONT'D) Kwisatz Haderach, show us the way!
YOU WILL SEE! THE BEAUTY AND THE HORROR!

chakobsa_translation: Abraanaha mwaddib gebreha! He, he, he!
english_dialogue: Muad'Dib likes his smell! Ha, ha, ha!
phonetic_transcription: a-BRAA-na-ha mwad-DIB GE-bre-ha! He, he, he! Likes desert-mouse his-smell! Ha, ha, ha!

chakobsa_translation: Hesshyaka che nazgahabit. Hesshyaha huraaha--hi kasshahaho. Hesshyahaho! Nifriha heshisha.
english_dialogue: You saw what he did. He saw it coming--and he saved her life. He saw it! He can foresee.
phonetic_transcription: HESH-shya-ka che NAZ-ga-ha-bit. HESH-shya-ha hu-RAA-ha--hi KASH-shaha-ho. HESH-shya-ha-ho! NIF-ri-ha HE-shi-sha.
translation_english: You-saw what he-did-it. He-saw it-coming--and he-saved-her. He-sawit! He-can foresee.

chakobsa_translation: Hinat ruyi uzzulatha. Shi ruk, faafa. Is-nata kiraalak taamrih zamaha. Hi so hadhaak hwishi Fraimin gasiig.
english_dialogue: Then I'll die. Maybe you will, too. But the others will keep going. And they won't stop until the Fremen are free.
phonetic_transcription: hi-nat RU-yi uz-ZU-la-tha. SHI RUK, FAA-fa. is-NA-ta ki-RAA-lak TAAMrih ZA-ma-ha. hi SO ha-DHAAK HWI-shi FRAI-min ga-SIIG.
translation_english: Then will I-die. Maybe you-will, too. But will-continue (the) others fighting. And not they-will-stop until Fremen are-free.

english_dialogue: Like Usul, the strength beneath the base of a pillar.

chakobsa_translation: E Mwaddib! So gommudhma! Jarma vahcha!
english_dialogue: Muad'Dib! Don't embarrass us! Call a big one!
phonetic_transcription: E mwad-DIB! SO GOM-mudh-ma! JAR-ma VAH-cha! O Desert-Mouse! Not embarrass-us! Call (a) large-one!

english_dialogue: Don't worry. Stilgar taught him well.
phonetic_transcription: SO CHIV-la-ho. LED-du-ha-ho CHE-shii STIL-gar. Not worry. Taught-him well Stilgar.
E mwad-DIB! VIIIIII!!! O DESERT-MOUSE! YEAAAAAH!!!

chakobsa_translation: Wa shaidah sekkaha Mwaddib fadla hi Shai Huluud!
english_dialogue: Muad'Dib finally made peace with Shai-Hulud!
phonetic_transcription: wa-SHAI-dah SEK-ka-ha mwad-DIB FAD-la hi SHAI hu-LUUD!
translation_english: Finally found Desert-Mouse peace with Shai Hulud!

chakobsa_translation: Jarmaha noqcho juugo. Vaahaiza hesshyahabit unij a-vadzih.
english_dialogue: He called a grandfather worm. The biggest anyone's ever seen.
phonetic_transcription: JAR-ma-ha NOQ-cho JUU-go. vaa-HAI-za HESH-shya-ha-bit U-nij a-vadZIH.
translation_english: He-called (a) worm grandfather. (The) biggest has-seen-that anyone ever.

english_dialogue: Shai-Hulud knows the coming of the storm.
Bless the Maker and his works.

chakobsa_translation: So vii ru Shaya Huluud leda Mahda hu huraaha?
english_dialogue: Will not Shai-Hulud know the Mahdi when he comes?
phonetic_transcription: SO VII ru SHA-ya hu-LUUD LE-da MAH-da hu hu-RAA-ha?
translation_english: Not is-it will Shai-Hulud know Mahdi when he-comes?

english_dialogue: Is everything all right, Reverend Mother?
Will you always be with me?
What do you think of him?
phonetic_transcription: is-KIF-tha CHAI-ya o-MAA-kath-sha-ho? Way what you-value-him?

english_dialogue: If He comes, you must welcome Him.

chakobsa_translation: Bii un-nukim biibii. Un-gifi oziig zamaak junju hwishi uzzulaathak.
english_dialogue: Only one at a time. Otherwise they fight each other to death.
phonetic_transcription: BII un-NU-kim BII-bii. un-GI-fi o-ZIIG za-MAAK JUN-ju hwi-shi uz-zuLAA-thak.
translation_english: One at-a-time only. Otherwise they-fight each-other until they-die.

chakobsa_translation: Marza cheshii. Noj erach, ru redha hurana a-minzimash. Ish ruqaahashii anzaga ogrog.
english_dialogue: Listen carefully. Soon, a man will visit your Temple. He may want you to perform the rite.
phonetic_transcription: MAR-za CHE-shii. no-JE-rach, ru RE-dha HU-ra-na a-MIN-zi-mash. ISH ru-QAA-ha-shii AN-za-ga OG-rog.
translation_english: Listen carefully. Soon, will (a) man visit your-temple. May he-askyou to-perform (the) rite.

english_dialogue: No, I can't perform the rite.

phonetic_transcription: QITH-na-ka RII-dhi a-FII--uz-ZUL-tha-ka HIIL. Have-tried men many--have-died all.

english_dialogue: I lost them all in the fire.

chakobsa_translation: Tassasak saich hiil un-yuchkim. Unaaraha jarmik rii qabgab deqah unnoqchim. E Mwaddib. Tamaakshii.
english_dialogue: All Northern Sietches have been hit. There is a call for a war council in the south. Muad'Dib. They're waiting for you.
phonetic_transcription: TAS-sa-sak SAICH HIIL un-YUCH-kim. u-NAA-ra-ha JAR-mik rii QAB-gab DE-qah un-NOQ-chim. E mwad-DIB. ta-MAAK-shii.
translation_english: Have-been-hit Sietches all in-the-north. There-is (a) call for (a) council of-war in-the-south. O Muad'Dib. They-are-waiting-for-you.

chakobsa_translation: So nifrish atmara zih, e Mwaddib! Isaadhak ziihij savi hu so isaadhash!
english_dialogue: You can't stay here Muad'Dib! None of these people will leave if you don't leave!
phonetic_transcription: SO NIF-rish AT-ma-ra ZIH, e mwad-DIB! i-SAA-dhak ZII-hij SA-vi hu SO i-SAA-dhash!
translation_english: Not you-can stay here, O Muad'Dib! They-will-leave these-people noneof if not you-leave!

chakobsa_translation: So oshii una zih, e leksit. Isa hu uzzulaash.
english_dialogue: You're not allowed in here, stranger. Leave or die.
phonetic_transcription: SO O-shii U-na ZIH, e LEK-sit. I-sa hu uz-zu-LAASH.
translation_english: Not for-you to-be here, stranger. Leave or die.

english_dialogue: If you are the One-TRANSLATION Hu she Chausij-PHONETIC hu SHE CHAU-sij-If you (are) the-One-------------------------------------------------------------------(MKEEPER_DUNE2_130A.mp3) MAKER KEEPER God is waiting for you.

chakobsa_translation: Hu zaihaash, rush uzzulatha. Hu zaihaash, shi heshyaash.
english_dialogue: If you drink, you shall die. If you drink, you may see.
phonetic_transcription: HU ZAI-haash, rush uz-ZU-la-tha. HU ZAI-haash, shi HESH-yaash.
translation_english: If you-drink, you-will die. If you drink, may you-see.

phonetic_transcription: le-DAA-ha CHE rii ZIH-ki? Knows who about this?
She BII-bii, e KA-lidh. You only, O Reverend-Mother.

english_dialogue: Good. What do you make of it?
phonetic_transcription: che-SHII. mi-NAASH CHE RII-ho? Good. Are-you-thinking what about-it?

chakobsa_translation: Ru vaija ho Chausij. Ru Lisaan al-Gayib huratha a-chem jillaha so redhi a-bit.
english_dialogue: Because he's the One. The Lisan al-Gaib should find his way to where no man has ever been.
phonetic_transcription: ru-VAI-ja ho CHAU-sij. ru li-SAAN al-GA-yib HU-ra-tha a-CHEM JIL-laha SO RE-dhi a-bit.
translation_english: Because he (is) the-One. Should Lisan al-Gaib find-his-way to-where has-gone no man to-there.

english_dialogue: He shall come back from the dead with Desert Spring tears.
Bring me a drop of Water of Life.
phonetic_transcription: NAZ-dha-nii ek-KER-cha EK-ke-ra ED-de-ma. Bring-me drop of-Water of-Life.

english_dialogue: Do you smash a knife before going to BATTLE?
phonetic_transcription: VII ju-RAAK DIM-bi wak ZA-ma-ha? Do you-smash knife before-you BATTLE?

chakobsa_translation: Unara sot un-taufih zihi nifribit qummunii.
english_dialogue: There is no one in this room who can stand against me.
phonetic_transcription: U-na-ra SOT un-TAU-fih ZI-hi NIF-ri-bit QUM-mu-nii.
translation_english: There-is no-one in-room this can-who stand-against-me.

chakobsa_translation: Qaubaka kaalik akaa rii hurayi. Jaha ish.
english_dialogue: Your mothers warned you about my coming. Fear the moment.
phonetic_transcription: QAU-ba-ka KAA-lik a-KAA rii HU-ra-yi. JA-ha ish.
translation_english: Warned-they mothers-your you about my-coming. Fear (the) moment.

chakobsa_translation: E she minaazash nifrish qira sahiim zaihadh.
english_dialogue: You think you could have a chance.
phonetic_transcription: e SHE mi-NAA-zash NI-frish QI-ra sa-HIIM ZAI-hadh.
translation_english: And you think you-can stand as champion.

chakobsa_translation: Is-nata jahaash: Shi ho Chausij? Shi zihik ishi tammashabit o eddemash hiili. Ruqaazash vadzih--kalagash--uzzulthabit nairgevi barthaa wadzih. Hardhaha uuth. Jurraha gari yimash neril yaqaalaha Jeske obit. Sayyaha airhur zaad hi thaa nishi vannaha obit. O erle chausi, jaraamas-ha abzih zihi sa kyurtu Fremnii--ho Lekis.
english_dialogue: But you're afraid: What if he could be the One? This could be the moment you've been waiting for all your life. You're praying now--to your grandmother--who passed away nine moons ago. She lost an eye. A rock had smashed her face as she was crossing the Belt. She was twelve when it happened. At that time, this world had a Fremen name-Dune.
phonetic_transcription: is-NA-ta ja-HAASH: SHI HO CHAU-sij? SHI ZI-hik I-shi TAM-ma-sha-bit o ED-de-mash HII-li. ru-QAA-zash vad-ZIH--KA-la-gash--uz-ZUL-tha-bit NAIR-ge-vi BAR-thaa wad-ZIH. HAR-dha-ha UUTH. JUR-ra-ha GA-ri YImash ne-ril ya-QAA-la-ha JES-ke o-bit. SAY-ya-ha AIR-hur ZAAD hi THAA ni-shi VAN-na-ha o-bit. o ER-le CHAU-si, ja-RAA-mas-ha AB-zih ZI-hi sa KYUR-tu FREM-nii--ho LE-kis.
translation_english: But you-are-afraid: Maybe he (is) the-One? Perhaps this-one (is) (the) moment you-have-waited-for-which during life-your all. You-arepraying now--to-your-grandmother--who-died at-months nine ago. Shelost (an) eye. It-had-smashed (a) rock her-face at-the-time she-wascrossing (the) Belt during-it. She-had-walked years twelve at-thatmoment it-occurred during-it. During time that, it-was-being-called world this by name Fremen--it-was Dune.

chakobsa_translation: Un-faisifash is-ruzam, dhikaash ekker a-zulhu. Hi anzaadhaha chimbi hukrash.
english_dialogue: In your nightmares, you give water to the dead. And it brings joy to your heart.
phonetic_transcription: un-FAI-si-fash is-RU-zam, dhi-KAASH EK-ker a-ZUL-hu. hi an-ZAA-dhaha CHIM-bi hu-krash.
translation_english: In-nightmares-your, you-give water to-the-dead. And it-brings joy toyour-heart.

chakobsa_translation: E Rudhi Dina, heshidhanii: ne Lisaan al-Gayib. Ruyi dimala ruk aSHIDHGIM.
english_dialogue: The Hand of God be my witness, I am the Voice from the Outer World. I will lead you to PARADISE.
phonetic_transcription: e RU-dhi DI-na, HE-shi-dha-nii: ne li-SAAN al-GA-yib. ru-yi DI-ma-la ruk a-SHIDH-gim.
translation_english: O Hand of-God, witness-me: I (am) Lisan al-Gaib. I-will lead you toParadise.

english_dialogue: A mighty one, Muad'Dib! A great grandmother of a storm.
phonetic_transcription: O-jij, e mwad-DIB! AF-shil al-KA-lig. A-mighty-one, O Desert-Mouse! (A) storm great-grandmother.
RUM GAS-su LEK-se. Let's free Dune.

chakobsa_translation: Dinla thoddhat Hozjom. Vizdha Sardaakra. Dhika than Dhonaj a-reshme.
english_dialogue: Bring the prisoners to the Residency. Kill the Sardaukar. Give the Baron's body to the desert.
phonetic_transcription: DIN-la THODH-dhat HOZ-jom. VIZ-dha sar-DAA-kra. DHI-ka THAN DHO-naj a-RESH-me.
translation_english: Bring prisoners to-Residency. Kill (the) Sardaukar. Give (the) body of-the-Baron to-the-desert.

phonetic_transcription: CHAR-ra, e mwad-dib. Yes, O Desert-Mouse.

chakobsa_translation: So ledaaraha yaqala! Qiraashaha Stilgar uzzulluth-ho. Hesshyuuhama * gif chaiyi?
english_dialogue: XXX * CHANI He doesn't know how to cross! Stilgar is going to get him killed. What is he trying to prove?
phonetic_transcription: SO le-DAA-ra-ha YA-qa-la! qi-RAA-sha-ha STIL-gar uz-ZUL-luth-ho. hesh-SHYUU-ha-ma GIF CHAI-yi?
translation_english: Not knows-how-he to-cross! Is-about-to Stilgar get-him-killed. To- * prove-to-us what? (Expression)

chakobsa_translation: Ruha ledaga geftha resham. Minaazii ho a-cheshhe. (off Chani's look) * Ru-che? Abraachashho vadzih?
english_dialogue: He shall know the ways of the desert. I think it's all for the best. * (off Chani's look) What? You care for him now?
phonetic_transcription: RU-ha LE-da-ga GEF-tha RE-sham. mi-NAA-zii a-CHESH-he. (off Chani's * look) ru-CHE? a-BRAA-chash-ho vad-zih?
translation_english: He-shall know (the) ways of-the-desert. I-think it (is) to-thegreater-good. (off Chani's look) What? You-care-for-him now?

chakobsa_translation: Ho so sahiim laiksat oziiyag. Minaazii ho vejii.
english_dialogue: He's not like the other strangers. I feel he's sincere.
phonetic_transcription: ho SO sa-HIIM LAIK-sat o-ZII-yag. mi-NAA-zii HO ve-JII.
translation_english: He not (is) like strangers others. I-believe he (is) sincere.
